description.task: Implement optimized Bubble Sort with an early-exit flag. If no swaps occur in a pass, the array is already sorted and the algorithm can terminate early. Print the array after each pass.
instructions: For arr2={1,2,3,4,5}: Pass 1 makes no swaps → early exit after 1 pass
Early exit optimization avoids unnecessary passes on sorted/nearly-sorted arrays
description.hint: if(arr[j]>arr[j+1]){int t=arr[j];arr[j]=arr[j+1];arr[j+1]=t;swapped=true;} and after inner loop: if(!swapped) break;

description.task: Implement Selection Sort that finds the minimum element in each unsorted pass and swaps it into position. Track and print the minimum index found in each pass.
instructions: Swap is already provided: temp=arr[i]; arr[i]=arr[minIdx]; arr[minIdx]=temp;
Pass 1: min is 11 at index 4 → swap with index 0 → [11,25,12,22,64]
Pass 2: min is 12 at index 2 → swap with index 1 → [11,12,25,22,64]
Selection sort always makes exactly n-1 swaps (unlike bubble sort)
description.hint: if(arr[j]<arr[minIdx]) minIdx=j; The swap is already written — just complete the inner loop.

description.task: Implement Insertion Sort that builds the sorted portion one element at a time by shifting elements right to make room. Show the partially sorted array after each insertion.
instructions: Shift loop: while(j>=0 && arr[j]>key){ arr[j+1]=arr[j]; j--; }
Place key: arr[j+1]=key; (after the while loop)
Starting arr={12,11,13,5,6}: key=11: shift 12 right → [11,12,13,5,6]; key=13: no shift; etc.
Print happens AFTER placing key, so output shows the state post-insertion
Insertion sort is efficient for nearly-sorted arrays and small n
